message.content: An adversary may create a snapshot or data backup within a cloud account to evade defenses. A snapshot is a point-in-time copy of an existing cloud compute component such as a virtual machine (VM), virtual hard drive, or volume. By leveraging permissions to create a snapshot, adversaries can bypass restrictions that prevent access to existing compute service infrastructure. This technique allows them to create a cloud instance, mount the snapshot, and apply policies that grant them access, such as inbound and outbound SSH access through a firewall policy. The potential impact includes unauthorized access to sensitive data, persistence within the cloud environment, and evasion of detection mechanisms. Common exploitation methods involve abusing cloud permissions and misconfigurations. Mitigation strategies include enforcing strict access controls, regularly auditing cloud permissions, and monitoring for unusual snapshot creation activities.
